DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances Via ZOOM
Matthew K. Davis, Esq. Law Office of Matthew K. Davis 805-464-6005 Attorney for Plaintiffs DAVID TERRY and JILL TERRY
FRANK J. PERRETTA, Esq., MESSNER REEVES LLP (408) 298-7120 fperretta@messner.com Attorney for Defendant FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION
Gina Minnis, CSR #11996 408-313-8720 gina.csr@gmail.com Court reporter.

Hearing held. Discussed, not argued. Counsel for defendant seeks clarification of the Court's tentative ruling. Court provides clarification.

DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Federal Express Corporation's demurrer to all five causes of action in the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") filed by plaintiffs David and Jill Terry is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for malicious prosecution and overruled as to all other causes of action.

Defendant's statute of limitations argument is not amenable on a demurrer. The FAC alleges that the parties tolled the statute of limitations and the action is timely. (FAC, pars. 45-48.) The court takes that allegation as true. Defendant improperly relies on extrinsic evidence (a declaration and tolling agreement) on demurrer. (E-Fab, Inc. v. Accountants, Inc. Services (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1308, 1315-1316 ["In order for the bar of the statute of limitations to be raised by demurrer, the defect must clearly and affirmatively appear on the face of the complaint..."].) "The hearing on demurrer may not be turned into a contested evidentiary hearing," especially where the meaning of an agreement is disputable. (Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 114.)

The demurrer to the malicious prosecution claim is sustained without leave to amend. An arrest alone cannot support the claim. Plaintiffs concede that the cause of action fails.

Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts to support the causes of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress and intentional infliction of emotional distress. Pitman v. City of Oakland (1988) 197 Cal.App.3d 1037 and Bogard v. Employers Casualty Co. (1985) 164 Cal.App.3d 602 do not compel a different conclusion. In Pitman the court explained that "[b]eing dismissed from a job is not an uncommon occurrence in modern society." (Pitman, 197 Cal.App.3d at 1047.) Bogard was a bad faith action. The facts alleged in the FAC are distinguishable as the fight and arrest support the claim and plaintiffs sufficiently allege their severe emotional distress. (FAC, par. 72 ["Plaintiffs, and each of them, in fact suffered severe emotional distress including, but not limited to, fright, grief, shame, anger, mortification and worry..."].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk; W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Gina Minnis, CSR #11996 gina.csr@gmail.com , Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew K. Davis, Esq. Law Office of Matthew K. Davis 805-464-6005 Attorney for Plaintiffs DAVID TERRY and JILL TERRY (via ZOOM)
FRANK J. PERRETTA, Esq., MESSNER REEVES LLP (408) 298-7120 fperretta@messner.com Attorney for Defendant FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION (ZOOM)
Gina Minnis, CSR #11996 408-313-8720 gina.csr@gmail.com Court reporter. (ZOOM)

Motion is not contested. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Federal Express Corporation's motion to strike portions of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is granted without leave to amend. No opposition filed. The portions of the first amended complaint seeking punitive damages and attorney's fees and alleging an amount of compensatory damages are stricken.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Matthew K. Davis, Esq. 805-464-6005 for Plaintiff.
Frank Perreta, Esq. 408-298-7120 for Defendant.
Court reporter: Kyle Sheperd, CSR #13756, 714-873-7217, kylesheperdcsr@gmail.com, reported.

ARGUED. Mr. Perreta contests the tentative ruling and argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Off calendar. The parties failed to meet and confer in compliance with CCP 430.41. Plaintiff's counsel is immediately ordered to meet and confer with defense counsel. Defendant Federal Express Corporation's response to the complaint is now due by February 21, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Kyle Sheperd, CSR #13756, 714-873-7217, kylesheperdcsr@gmail.com, reported.(302/JMQ)
